message:
feat(analysis,tui): expand open-like status recognition and fix hardcoded recency timestamp

The status model previously used a positive allowlist (open, in_progress,
blocked, ready) to identify "open" issues. This missed newer workflow
statuses like review, deferred, pinned, and hooked — causing them to be
silently treated as closed in file-intel lookups, recipes, and forecasts.

Changes:
- file_intel: Invert is_open_status to use negative match (closed/tombstone)
  so any new status is treated as open by default; add impact weight for
  new statuses; add test for review status recognition
- forecast: Use Issue::is_closed_like() for velocity calculation instead of
  string comparison, ensuring tombstone issues count toward velocity samples;
  add test verifying tombstone is counted
- recipe: Add open_like_status_filters() helper with all 7 open-like
  statuses; apply to default, high-impact, and stale builtin recipes; make
  status filter comparison case-insensitive with trim; add tests for new
  status inclusion and case-insensitivity
- search: Replace hardcoded "2026-03-04" timestamp in normalize_recency()
  with Utc::now() so recency scoring works correctly regardless of date;
  add test asserting current timestamps score > 0.9
- tui: Import Write trait for flush support
- beads: Close bd-155 (test isolation issue resolved)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
